title: Define behavior for split Agent Misrepresentation decision-element and composite scores
description:
# Feature

Realign the split Agent Misrepresentation scores on the SelectQuote HCS Medium-Risk scorecard to the Information Accuracy rubric while keeping the legacy score in place.

# Scenario: Composite passes when no phrase score fails

Given phrase-level Agent Misrepresentation scores exist for High-Pressure Tactics, Copay Guarantees, Savings Promises, Ambiguous Cost Language, False Carrier Claims, False Local Pharmacy Claims, and False Service Claims
When a call transcript is evaluated and each phrase-level score returns Yes
Then the composite score returns Yes
And the composite explanation indicates that all phrase-level elements passed

# Scenario: Composite fails when one phrase score fails

Given the CSV-aligned split Agent Misrepresentation scores exist
When a transcript causes exactly one phrase-level score to return No
Then the composite score returns No
And the composite explanation names that failed phrase score

# Scenario: Composite explanation concatenates multiple failed phrases in rubric order

Given the CSV-aligned split Agent Misrepresentation scores exist
When a transcript causes multiple phrase-level scores to return No
Then the composite score returns No
And the composite explanation concatenates the failed phrase score names in the rubric row order

# Scenario: Legacy score remains available

Given the original Agent Misrepresentation score exists on the scorecard
When the CSV-aligned split scores are added or repurposed
Then the original Agent Misrepresentation score remains present and unchanged
